data.title: Australia's EML Payments falls as Jefferies cuts PT on weak quarterly update
data.description:
** Shares of EML Payments fall as much as 2% to A$0.858

** Jefferies cuts price target for EML to A$1 per share from A$1.40 after weak first-quarter trading updates

** Brokerage cites lower market risk appetite following the update as a reason for PT downgrade; retains "buy" rating

** The payment solutions provider reported a 42% drop in underlying EBITDA for first quarter and 2% decline in total revenue on Wednesday

** Jefferies sees normalisation of rate cut cycles as a potential headwind for EML

data.title: Palo Alto Networks' earnings fail to wow Wall Street - even with a new AI deal
data.description:
By Emily Bary

The cybersecurity company's stock has lagged the S&P 500 this year, and that underperformance could continue after its latest results

Palo Alto Networks says its "platformization" strategy is paying dividends.

Nvidia Corp. wasn't the only technology company on Wednesday's earnings docket, as investors also just got the latest results from Palo Alto Networks Inc.

Shares of the cybersecurity company have lagged the S&P 500 SPX this year, reflecting a general struggle for momentum within the software sector. And that underperformance looks set to extend, with Palo Alto Networks shares (PANW) down more than 4% in the extended session Wednesday.

The company's revenue forecast essentially met Wall Street's expectations: It expects total revenue of $2.57 billion to $2.59 billion for the fiscal second quarter, while analysts tracked by FactSet were modeling $2.58 billion.

Read: Microsoft is at risk of falling behind Google in this measure of tech supremacy

Palo Alto Networks is also looking for 93 cents to 95 cents in adjusted earnings per share, versus the consensus view of 93 cents. The comapany is targeting $15.75 billion to $15.85 billion in remaining performance obligations, a measure of its backlog; analysts had been looking for $15.78 billion.

Chief Executive Nikesh Arora said Palo Alto Networks has been benefiting from its "platformization" push. The company sought to get customers to adopt more of its products by offering additional services at a discount. That strategy has been the source of some investor consternation, but management says it's paying off.

For the fiscal first quarter, revenue amounted to $2.47 billion, up 16% from a year before, while analysts were looking for $2.46 billion. Annual recurring revenue from next-generation security offerings grew even faster, at 29%. And Palo Alto Networks' remaining performance obligations totaled $15.5 billion, up 24% from a year earlier.

Don't miss: Alphabet's stunning stock surge this year could fuel this never-before-seen feat

"We continued to execute with excellence to start the year, as shown by our strong top-line growth and operating efficiency," Chief Financial Officer Dipak Golechha said in a release.

Palo Alto Networks also said it intends to purchase Chronosphere, a cybersecurity company focused on observability tools, for $3.35 billion.

"The foundational requirement for every modern AI data center is constant uptime and resilience, which demands real-time, always-on observability delivered at the right cost," Arora said in a separate release. "Chronosphere was built to scale for the data demands of the AI era from day one."

-Emily Bary

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Australia's Nufarm hits 3-month high; analysts raise PT on strong growth outlook
data.description:
** Australian agribusiness Nufarm Ltd rises as much as 11.4% to A$2.64, its highest since August 20

** Citi upgrades rating to 'Neutral' from 'Sell' to reflect better earnings growth outlook, raises PT to A$2.55 from A$2.35

** Macquarie analysts also raise PT to A$2.77 from A$2.55

** Co on Wednesday reported better-than-expected FY25 earnings, strong FY26 outlook, and named Rico Christensen as its new CEO

** Citi and Macquarie both say Christensen's appointment was "logical" is likely to be well received by the market given his extensive experience across agricultural industry

** "While balance sheet risk lingers, we are encouraged by more constructive growth trajectory and removal of the Seed Technologies overhang" — Citi

** Citi surprised by NUF's decision to hold onto seed technologies business instead of a sale or closure, but notes co's focus on reducing costs and capital requirements

** Citi says there could be upside to omega-3 earnings expectations if uptick in fish oil price in November persists

** As of last close, stock down 33.1% YTD

data.title: Base Metals Rise as Selling Pressure Stabilizes — Market Talk
data.description: Base metals rise in Asian trading. Recent selling pressures appear to have stabilized, say Sucden Financial analysts in a note. Despite a stronger U.S. dollar, sentiment is tracking equity markets, with key technical levels in major indices still intact, the team writes. Copper was likely also bolstered by improved sentiment on a report from Chile's copper agency Cochilco, which is now expecting an improved price environment, says ANZ Research. The three-month copper contract on the LME is up 0.5% at $10,802.50 a ton, aluminum is up 0.7% and zinc is 0.5% higher. (megan.cheah@wsj.com)

data.title: Stocks rally hard after Nvidia tops estimates, jobs data in view
data.description:
Key points:
  • Nikkei surges 3.9%, Kospi gains 2.8%, S&P futures up 1.2%
  • Nvidia up 4.9% after-hours after forecasting revenue well above estimates
  • Fed rate-cut odds fall with delayed jobs data in focus

By Gregor Stuart Hunter

A relief rally swept across Asian markets and lifted stocks in early trading on Thursday as investors cheered Nvidia's market-topping earnings, while the dollar rose as traders braced for the release of delayed jobs data.

MSCI's broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan (.MIAPJ0000PUS) was up 0.6%, rebounding from a one-month low, after Nvidia forecast quarterly revenue well above Wall Street estimates on Wednesday. CEO Jensen Huang touted blockbuster demand for its AI chips from giant cloud providers and shrugged off concerns about an AI bubble.

S&P 500 e-mini futures rose 1.1%.

Nvidia "delivered yet another master class in AI dominance," said Tony Sycamore, market analyst at IG in Sydney.

Stocks on Wall Street had snapped a four-day losing streak on Wednesday before the earnings release. All three major indexes rebounded from the selloff as the world's most valuable company's earnings report tempered the AI valuation fears that had triggered the rout.

The U.S. dollar index , which tracks the greenback's strength against a basket of six major peers, advanced 0.1% to 100.17, hovering close to a two-week high.

The yield on benchmark 10-year Treasury notes rose to 4.1444% compared with its U.S. close of 4.131% on Wednesday.

Traders are awaiting the release of September's delayed jobs report, due for release later in the global day, to provide clues on the Federal Reserve's next move.

Minutes from the Fed's October meeting released on Wednesday showed it cut interest rates even as policymakers cautioned that doing so could risk entrenched inflation and a loss of public trust in the U.S. central bank.

Fed funds futures are pricing an implied 33% probability of a 25-basis-point cut at the next meeting on December 10, down from a 50% chance a day earlier, according to the CME Group's FedWatch tool.

An updated schedule for the release of the November jobs report, now delayed until December 16, is behind the move, said Gavin Friend, senior markets strategist at National Australia Bank in London.

"That's six days after the December FOMC meeting, and that's why the 12 or 13 basis points of rate cuts that were priced in for December, 50% or so, has been immediately evaporated," he said on a podcast. From the market's perspective, he said, the data fog "plays to the Fed's messaging that 'we need to pause'."

The dollar dropped 0.2% against the yen to 156.92 , after the Japanese currency reached its weakest level in ten months during U.S. trading hours, and set a record-low against the euro .

Against the dollar, the European single currency was 0.1% weaker on the day at $1.1530.

Brent crude was steady at $63.51 per barrel.

Cryptocurrencies retraced a recent selloff, with bitcoin and ether both up 1.6% each.

Gold advanced 0.7% to $4,108.22 per ounce.
